name: La Cantine
why: Poblano egg & cheese sandwich. 5-min walk from Airbnb. Best cheap pre-night-out fuel.

name: Tacos El Bronco
why: Al pastor, suadero, lengua. Late counter, proper pre-techno fuel.

name: Roberta's
why: Wood-fired pizza institution. Carb load option near Airbnb.

name: Bunna Cafe
why: Ethiopian vegan, injera platter. Walking from Airbnb. Light reset meal.

name: Win Son Bakery
why: Scallion pancake breakfast sandos, mochi donuts. Taiwanese-American. Walking from Airbnb. Proper persona.

name: Kellogg's Diner
why: Revived 1928 diner, Jackie Carnesi. Nostalgic format, serious cooking underneath.

name: Frankel's Delicatessen
why: Pastrami egg & cheese, whitefish bagels. Real Jewish deli energy.

name: Birds of a Feather
why: Sichuan. Mapo tofu, dan dan noodles, dry-fried green beans. Walking from Airbnb.

name: Bonnie's
why: Calvin Eng's Cantonese-American. Pork floss McFlurry. One of the most talked-about NYC openings.

name: Lighthouse
why: Mediterranean meze. Easy landing after a long day. Consistent, solid.

name: Sunday in Brooklyn
why: Hazelnut praline pancakes. Brunch institution.

name: Court Street Grocers
why: Vegitalian combo sandwich. Rosner-approved. Counter sandwich as art form.

name: Wenwen
why: Taiwanese. BDSM Chicken, $69 Long Island Iced Tea. Persona absolutely loaded. Walking to Kim Petras venue.

name: Strange Delight
why: New Orleans-inflected. Blackened swordfish belly. Ham El-Waylly. Rosner best dishes 2024.

name: Sukh
why: Surat Thani Thai. Train-car interior. Same block as Strange Delight — pick one. Khao khai ra-bert.

name: Café Mado
why: Nico Russell (Place des Fêtes team). Jambon-beurre + seasonal. Rosner best dishes 2024. All-day flexibility.

name: Place des Fêtes
why: Nico Russell. Rosner called it 'heaven.' Natural wine. Neighbourhood restaurant as it should be.

name: Bong
why: Cambodian whole-fried fish. Rosner best of 2025. Serious destination dinner.
booking: Book — Resy at midnight, 20 days out

name: Sailor
why: Rosner full feature. Persona-driven seasonal. One of the stronger NYC restaurant openings in recent memory.

name: Cervo's
why: Iberian seafood, sister to Hart's. Natural wine. Walk-in friendly. Perfect Sunday LES lunch.

name: Mắm
why: Vietnamese, sidewalk plastic stools. Fermented shrimp paste focus. Casual, loud, delicious.

name: Ha's Snack Bar
why: Bon Appétit #1 NYC 2025. Counter only. Closed Sun + Mon — doesn't fit this trip. Future visit.

name: Russ & Daughters Cafe
why: Smoked fish sit-down. NYC institution. Sunday brunch on the LES.

name: Bánh Anh Em
why: Pate banh mi — Rosner's best sandwich 2025. Pork floss. Close to Tompkins for Drag March.

name: Superiority Burger
why: Brooks Headley's legendary vegetarian counter. Small, quick, always good.

name: Veselka
why: 24hr Ukrainian diner. Pierogi, borscht. Pride Sunday fallback.

name: Dean's
why: Jess Shadbolt + Annie Shi (King team). British seafood pub. Oysters, langoustines, Guinness on tap. Pre-FIST dinner.
booking: Book — Resy 2 wks ahead, or walk-in bar

name: Mommy Pai's
why: Filet O'Tofu sandwich. Taiwanese-American takeout counter. Rosner-approved.

name: Zimmi's
why: Provençal. Chicken liver tagliatelle. Rosner highlight. Neighbourhood charm, serious cooking.

name: Buvette
why: French café. Croque madame, eggs, natural wine. Near Pride March endpoint Sun 28.

name: Passerine
why: Maharashtrian. Blue corn bhakri. Sietsema 2024 standout. 15 min walk to Tompkins for Drag March.

name: S&P Lunch
why: Russ & Daughters diner-counter reinvention. 5 min from Pride March start. Memorable last-day brunch.

name: Cocina Consuelo
why: Karina Garcia. Salsa macha grilled cheese on croissant. On A train route south from Cloisters.

name: Theodora
why: Tomer Blechman. Mediterranean wood-fire. Reservation required weeks out.

name: Laila
why: Syrian. Sietsema 'most memorable meal 2024.' On the Q line from Coney Island.

name: Hainan Chicken House
why: Family-owned Hainanese chicken. Pete Wells big surprise pick 2024.

name: Sawa
why: Lebanese. Kibbeh nayeh (raw lamb). Serious Middle Eastern cooking.

name: JR & Son
why: Reinvented chicken parm, sesame-seeded. Brooklyn neighbourhood restaurant with a real point of view.

name: Nathan's Famous
why: 1916 original. NYC obligation. Hot dog as pilgrimage.

name: Ruby's Bar
why: 1934 boardwalk dive. Beer + chowder. Perfect post-parade stop.

name: Totonno's Pizzeria
why: 1924 coal-oven. One of NYC's oldest pizzerias. Verify hours.

name: Skovorodka
why: Pierogi-heavy. Local Russian institution. No frills.

name: Mermaid Spa restaurant
why: Dinner in robes post-banya. Borscht, pelmeni, kotleti, vodka.

name: Riis Park Beach Bazaar
why: On-site at Riis. Tacos, lobster rolls, beer. Best beach lunch without bringing your own.

name: Rockaway Beach Surf Club
why: Tacos + beer, further east. Surfer-crowd energy.

name: Lil' Deb's Oasis
why: Tropical comfort food. Queer-owned, persona-loaded. One of the most exciting restaurants in the Hudson Valley.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: Mezze Bistro + Bar
why: Mediterranean prix fixe, chef-led, local ingredients. Trip's one Berkshires splurge.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: No Comply Foods
why: Daily-changing menu, no tips, no reservations. Skater-punk ethos, serious cooking. Bon Appétit top US. 30 min south on return route.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: Blue Mango
why: Thai-Japanese. Reliable, walkable from Hotel Downstreet. Good casual post-hike dinner.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: STATE Food + Drink
why: Vietnamese Tuesdays at Greylock WORKS. Event-driven — verify statefoodanddrink.com before going.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: Mission Bar + Tapas
why: Queer-owned. Small plates, walking from Hotel Downstreet. Backup Mon or Tue dinner.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: Tunnel City Coffee
why: Specialty roaster. Pack the summit lunch here. Also on-site Wed at MASS MoCA.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: Bluebird & Co
why: Same Mezze hospitality group. Mon dinner Plan B only if arriving before 9pm.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: Trail House Kitchen
why: 2 min from MASS MoCA. Seasonal, outdoor patio in summer. Good Wed lunch.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: A-OK Berkshire BBQ
why: Texas-style BBQ in western MA. Fast, cheap, satisfying mid-day fuel.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive

name: Brewhaha Coffee
why: Best coffee near Hotel Downstreet. Pastries, breakfast sandwiches.
section: Berkshires / Hudson Valley — on the drive
